visa_types.name: Austrian National Visa (Visa D)
visa_types.documents_required:
Important: Review the steps to apply carefully to ensure you submit a completed application set.
Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Click here (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-ottawa/travel-information-on-austria/entrance-and-residence/visa/).
For Minors: The consent form must be signed by both parents on the day of the appointment at the Visa Application Center.
Document Checklist
Application for Austrian National Visa (Visa D)
The jurisdiction in Canada is as follows:
1. Residents of:
- the National Capital Region of Ottawa or its counties (Lanark, Leeds and Grenville, Prescott and Russell, Renfrew, Stormont, Dundas and Glengarry)
- Quebec
- New Brunswick
- Newfoundland and Labrador
- Nova Scotia
- Nunavut
- Prince Edward Islands
- Applicants for Schengen Visa C traveling to Austria and/or Germany.
and
applicants for (Austrian) National Visa D, Visa C-Erwerb, Visa D-Erwerb
have to book an appointment at VFS Global – Ottawa Centre.

2. Residents of: 
- Manitoba
- Ontario, except residents of the National Capital Region of Ottawa or its counties (Lanark, Leeds and Grenville, Prescott and Russell, Renfrew, Stormont, Dundas and Glengarry)
- Applicants for Schengen Visa C traveling to Austria have to book an appointment at the German Consulate in Toronto, ON. (https://canada.diplo.de/ca-en/consular-services/visa)
- Applicants for (Austrian) National Visa D have to book an appointment at VFS Global – Ottawa Centre.

3. Residents of: 
- Alberta
- British Columbia
- Northwest Territories
- Saskatchewan
- Yukon Territory
- Applicants for Schengen Visa C traveling to Austria have to book an appointment at the Consulate General of Switzerland in Vancouver, BC. (https://www.eda.admin.ch/countries/canada/en/home/representations/embassy-in-ottawa/consulate-general-vancouver.html)
Applicants for (Austrian) National Visa D have to book an appointment at VFS Global – Vancouver or Edmonton.
- For latest updates
CLICK HERE (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-ottawa/travel-information-on-austria)
visa_types.visa_fees:
Visa Fee at Glance
Fees are subject to change without prior notice. If your application meets the initial checks upon submission, it will then be accepted for Visa payments at Visa Application Centre. Payments can be made via Debit/Credit cards only. 

[Table:  Austrian National Visa (Visa D)]
Service Charge

[Table:  Austrian National Visa (Visa D)]
VALUE ADDED SERVICES

[Table:  Austrian National Visa (Visa D)]
Please Note:
All the charges are non-refundable, even if the application is refused or withdrawn.
Applicants are requested to collect appropriate receipts for all the payments.
After Embassy/Consulate verifies the documents submitted by the applicant, the Embassy/Consulate holds the right to ask for additional documents depending on the case if required.
If your visa is granted then it is the applicant's responsibility to check all the content of the visa issued to ensure it covers the period of stay requested, the number of entries required, the applicant details and that it is valid for the purpose of the trip.
visa_types.processing_time:
- Processing time for visa applications is minimum 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted.
- This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate.
- During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least three weeks prior to the date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.
- Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
visa_types.forms:
APPLICATION FOR AUSTRIAN NATIONAL VISA D

Minor applicants: Declaration of consent for the issuance of Visa (Austria) (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/fileadmin/user_upload/Vertretungen/Ottawa/Dokumente/Visa/Declaration_of_consent_Austria.pdf)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you intend to stay in Austria for more than 90 days and up to 6 months AND you are not a citizen of an European Union or European Free Trade Association member state, you need to apply for a national Austrian visa (Visa D).
Additional information for students and visiting scholars is available at the website of the Embassy of Austria (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-ottawa/).
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you intend to stay in Austria for more than 90 days and up to 6 months AND you are not a citizen of an European Union or European Free Trade Association member state, you need to apply for a national Austrian visa (Visa D).
Additional information for students and visiting scholars is available at the website of the Embassy of Austria (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-ottawa/).

## Visa Fees
Visa Fee at Glance
Fees are subject to change without prior notice. If your application meets the initial checks upon submission, it will then be accepted for Visa payments at Visa Application Centre. Payments can be made via Debit/Credit cards only. 

[Table:  Austrian National Visa (Visa D)]
Service Charge

[Table:  Austrian National Visa (Visa D)]
VALUE ADDED SERVICES

[Table:  Austrian National Visa (Visa D)]
Please Note:
All the charges are non-refundable, even if the application is refused or withdrawn.
Applicants are requested to collect appropriate receipts for all the payments.
After Embassy/Consulate verifies the documents submitted by the applicant, the Embassy/Consulate holds the right to ask for additional documents depending on the case if required.
If your visa is granted then it is the applicant's responsibility to check all the content of the visa issued to ensure it covers the period of stay requested, the number of entries required, the applicant details and that it is valid for the purpose of the trip.

## Documents Required
Important: Review the steps to apply carefully to ensure you submit a completed application set.
Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Click here (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-ottawa/travel-information-on-austria/entrance-and-residence/visa/).
For Minors: The consent form must be signed by both parents on the day of the appointment at the Visa Application Center.
Document Checklist
Application for Austrian National Visa (Visa D)
The jurisdiction in Canada is as follows:
1. Residents of:
- the National Capital Region of Ottawa or its counties (Lanark, Leeds and Grenville, Prescott and Russell, Renfrew, Stormont, Dundas and Glengarry)
- Quebec
- New Brunswick
- Newfoundland and Labrador
- Nova Scotia
- Nunavut
- Prince Edward Islands
- Applicants for Schengen Visa C traveling to Austria and/or Germany.
and
applicants for (Austrian) National Visa D, Visa C-Erwerb, Visa D-Erwerb
have to book an appointment at VFS Global – Ottawa Centre.

2. Residents of: 
- Manitoba
- Ontario, except residents of the National Capital Region of Ottawa or its counties (Lanark, Leeds and Grenville, Prescott and Russell, Renfrew, Stormont, Dundas and Glengarry)
- Applicants for Schengen Visa C traveling to Austria have to book an appointment at the German Consulate in Toronto, ON. (https://canada.diplo.de/ca-en/consular-services/visa)
- Applicants for (Austrian) National Visa D have to book an appointment at VFS Global – Ottawa Centre.

3. Residents of: 
- Alberta
- British Columbia
- Northwest Territories
- Saskatchewan
- Yukon Territory
- Applicants for Schengen Visa C traveling to Austria have to book an appointment at the Consulate General of Switzerland in Vancouver, BC. (https://www.eda.admin.ch/countries/canada/en/home/representations/embassy-in-ottawa/consulate-general-vancouver.html)
Applicants for (Austrian) National Visa D have to book an appointment at VFS Global – Vancouver or Edmonton.
- For latest updates
CLICK HERE (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-ottawa/travel-information-on-austria)

## Photo Specification
Please provide one recent (not older than 6 months) colour photograph of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
																					
- Taken against a plain light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Photograph attached (glued) to the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements (see examples here), then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application centre

## Processing Time
- Processing time for visa applications is minimum 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted.
- This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate.
- During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least three weeks prior to the date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.
- Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Forms
APPLICATION FOR AUSTRIAN NATIONAL VISA D

Minor applicants: Declaration of consent for the issuance of Visa (Austria) (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/fileadmin/user_upload/Vertretungen/Ottawa/Dokumente/Visa/Declaration_of_consent_Austria.pdf)

If your visa is granted then it is the applicant's responsibility to check all the content of the visa issued to ensure it covers the period of stay requested, the number of entries required, the applicant details and that it is valid for the purpose of the trip.

## Documents Required
Important: Review the steps to apply carefully to ensure you submit a completed application set.
All steps of the application process should be carefully reviewed to ensure that a complete and accurate set of documents is submitted.
Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Click here (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-ottawa/travel-information-on-austria/entrance-and-residence/visa/).

## Photo Specification

- Taken against a plain light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Photograph attached (glued) to the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements (see examples here), then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application centre

## Processing Time
- Processing time for visa applications is minimum 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted.
- This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate.
- During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least three weeks prior to the date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.
- Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

visa_types.name: Schengen Visa (Visa C)
visa_types.documents_required:
Important: Review the steps to apply carefully to ensure you submit a completed application set.
All steps of the application process should be carefully reviewed to ensure that a complete and accurate set of documents is submitted. Additionally, it is essential to review all jurisdiction-specific instructions and guidelines before proceeding with the appointment booking to ensure a smooth and efficient process.
The Schengen visa C is intended for touristic, business or visiting purposes (without gainful employment) for a maximum stay of 90 days per 180 days.
If a stay of more than 90 days is planned, you should apply for a visa D, which entitles the holder to stay in Austria up to six months.
Document Checklist
Application for Schengen Visa (Visa C)
The jurisdiction in Canada is as follows:
1. Residents of: 
- the National Capital Region of Ottawa or its counties (Lanark, Leeds and Grenville, Prescott and Russell, Renfrew, Stormont, Dundas and Glengarry)
- Quebec
- New Brunswick
- Newfoundland and Labrador
- Nova Scotia
- Nunavut
- Prince Edward Islands
- Applicants for Schengen Visa C traveling to Austria and/or Germany.
and
applicants for (Austrian) National Visa D, Visa C-Erwerb, Visa D-Erwerb
have to book an appointment at VFS Global – Ottawa Centre.

2. Residents of: 
- Manitoba
- Ontario, except residents of the National Capital Region of Ottawa or its counties (Lanark, Leeds and Grenville, Prescott and Russell, Renfrew, Stormont, Dundas and Glengarry)
- Applicants for Schengen Visa C traveling to Austria have to book an appointment at the
 German Consulate in Toronto, ON. (https://canada.diplo.de/ca-en/consular-services/visa)
- Applicants for (Austrian) National Visa D have to book an appointment at VFS Global – Ottawa Centre.

3. Residents of: 
- Alberta
- British Columbia
- Northwest Territories
- Saskatchewan
- Yukon Territory
- Applicants for Schengen Visa C traveling to Austria have to book an appointment at the
 Consulate General of Switzerland in Vancouver, BC. (https://www.eda.admin.ch/countries/canada/en/home/representations/embassy-in-ottawa/consulate-general-vancouver.html)
Applicants for (Austrian) National Visa D have to book an appointment at VFS Global – Vancouver or Edmonton.
Note:
For latest updates CLICK HERE (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-ottawa/travel-information-on-austria)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Schengen visa is valid for  Schengen member states, however the decision is sole discretion of Embassy/Consulate
The visa application cannot be submitted more than 6 months prior to departure and application has to be lodged not later than 15 business days before the intended start of the trip. Otherwise the application is inadmissible.
Applications are processed in the order of receipt.Processing usually takes approximately 15 business days from receipt by the Embassy of a complete application.
If further checks are necessary, the processing time may increase.to approximately 45 business days. This estimate excludes the shipping of applications to the Embassy and the return of passports to applicants.
The Embassy does NOT provide status updates on visa applications.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A Schengen visa is valid for  Schengen member states, however the decision is sole discretion of Embassy/Consulate
The visa application cannot be submitted more than 6 months prior to departure and application has to be lodged not later than 15 business days before the intended start of the trip. Otherwise the application is inadmissible.
Applications are processed in the order of receipt.Processing usually takes approximately 15 business days from receipt by the Embassy of a complete application.
If further checks are necessary, the processing time may increase.to approximately 45 business days. This estimate excludes the shipping of applications to the Embassy and the return of passports to applicants.
The Embassy does NOT provide status updates on visa applications.

## Visa Fees
Visa Fee at Glance
Fees are subject to change without prior notice. If your application meets the initial checks upon submission, it will then be accepted for Visa payments at Visa Application Centre. Payments can be made via Debit/Credit cards only. 

[Table:  Schengen Visa (Visa C)]

Applicants are requested to collect appropriate receipts for all the payments.
After Embassy/Consulate verifies the documents submitted by the applicant, the Embassy/Consulate holds the right to ask for additional documents depending on the case if required.
If your visa is granted then it is the applicant's responsibility to check all the content of the visa issued to ensure it covers the period of stay requested, the number of entries required, the applicant details and that it is valid for the purpose of the trip.

## Documents Required
Important: Review the steps to apply carefully to ensure you submit a completed application set.
All steps of the application process should be carefully reviewed to ensure that a complete and accurate set of documents is submitted. Additionally, it is essential to review all jurisdiction-specific instructions and guidelines before proceeding with the appointment booking to ensure a smooth and efficient process.
The Schengen visa C is intended for touristic, business or visiting purposes (without gainful employment) for a maximum stay of 90 days per 180 days.
If a stay of more than 90 days is planned, you should apply for a visa D, which entitles the holder to stay in Austria up to six months.

- Applicants for (Austrian) National Visa D have to book an appointment at VFS Global – Ottawa Centre.

Applicants for (Austrian) National Visa D have to book an appointment at VFS Global – Vancouver or Edmonton.
Note:
For latest updates CLICK HERE (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-ottawa/travel-information-on-austria)

## Photo Specification
Please provide one recent (not older than 6 months) colour photograph of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
																					
- Taken against a plain light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Photograph attached (glued) to the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements (see examples here), then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application centre

## Processing Time
- Processing time for visa applications is minimum 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted.
- This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate.
- During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least three weeks prior to the date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.

Applicants for (Austrian) National Visa D have to book an appointment at VFS Global – Vancouver or Edmonton.
- For latest updates
 CLICK HERE (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-ottawa/travel-information-on-austria)

After Embassy/Consulate verifies the documents submitted by the applicant, the Embassy/Consulate holds the right to ask for additional documents depending on the case if required.
If your visa is granted then it is the applicant's responsibility to check all the content of the visa issued to ensure it covers the period of stay requested, the number of entries required, the applicant details and that it is valid for the purpose of the trip.
visa_types.processing_time:
- Processing time for visa applications is minimum 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted.
- This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate.
- During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least three weeks prior to the date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.
- Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
In principle, foreigners who do not leave the international transit zone of an airport during a stopover, a flight segment or international flights do not require a visa.
However, nationals of the countries listed in the following require a visa (category “A”) also to stay in the international transit zone of an Austrian airport unless they are covered by an exemption:
Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Democratic Republic of Congo, Eritrea, Ethopia, Ghana, Iran, Iraq, Nigeria, Pakistan, Somalia, Sri Lanka, Syria
Further information on visa requirements and exemptions can be found on the website of the Federal Ministry of the Interior. CLICK HERE (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-ottawa/travel-information-on-austria/entrance-and-residence/visa)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
In principle, foreigners who do not leave the international transit zone of an airport during a stopover, a flight segment or international flights do not require a visa.
However, nationals of the countries listed in the following require a visa (category “A”) also to stay in the international transit zone of an Austrian airport unless they are covered by an exemption:
Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Democratic Republic of Congo, Eritrea, Ethopia, Ghana, Iran, Iraq, Nigeria, Pakistan, Somalia, Sri Lanka, Syria
Further information on visa requirements and exemptions can be found on the website of the Federal Ministry of the Interior. CLICK HERE (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-ottawa/travel-information-on-austria/entrance-and-residence/visa)

## Visa Fees
Visa Fee at Glance
Fees are subject to change without prior notice. If your application meets the initial checks upon submission, it will then be accepted for Visa payments at Visa Application Centre. Payments can be made via Debit/Credit cards only. 

[Table:  Airport Transit (Visa A)]
Service Charge

[Table:  Airport Transit (Visa A)]
VALUE ADDED SERVICES

[Table:  Airport Transit (Visa A)]
Please Note:
All the charges are non-refundable, even if the application is refused or withdrawn.
Applicants are requested to collect appropriate receipts for all the payments.
After Embassy/Consulate verifies the documents submitted by the applicant, the Embassy/Consulate holds the right to ask for additional documents depending on the case if required.
If your visa is granted then it is the applicant's responsibility to check all the content of the visa issued to ensure it covers the period of stay requested, the number of entries required, the applicant details and that it is valid for the purpose of the trip.

## Documents Required
Important:All steps of the application process should be carefully reviewed to ensure that a complete and accurate set of documents is submitted. Additionally, it is essential to review all jurisdiction-specific instructions and guidelines before proceeding with the appointment booking to ensure a smooth and efficient process. Click here (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-ottawa/travel-information-on-austria/entrance-and-residence/visa/). 
Airport Transit Visa A
Checklist
The jurisdiction in Canada is as follows:
1. Residents of: 
- the National Capital Region of Ottawa or its counties (Lanark, Leeds and Grenville, Prescott and Russell, Renfrew, Stormont, Dundas and Glengarry)
- Quebec
- New Brunswick
- Newfoundland and Labrador
- Nova Scotia
- Nunavut
- Prince Edward Islands
- Applicants for Schengen Visa C traveling to Austria and/or Germany.
and
applicants for (Austrian) National Visa D, Visa C-Erwerb, Visa D-Erwerb
have to book an appointment at VFS Global – Ottawa Centre.

2. Residents of: 
- Manitoba
- Ontario, except residents of the National Capital Region of Ottawa or its counties (Lanark, Leeds and Grenville, Prescott and Russell, Renfrew, Stormont, Dundas and Glengarry)
- Applicants for Schengen Visa C traveling to Austria have to book an appointment at the
 German Consulate in Toronto, ON. (https://canada.diplo.de/ca-en/consular-services/visa)
- Applicants for (Austrian) National Visa D have to book an appointment at VFS Global – Ottawa Centre.

3. Residents of: 
- Alberta
- British Columbia
- Northwest Territories
- Saskatchewan
- Yukon Territory
- Applicants for Schengen Visa C traveling to Austria have to book an appointment at the
 Consulate General of Switzerland in Vancouver, BC. (https://www.eda.admin.ch/countries/canada/en/home/representations/embassy-in-ottawa/consulate-general-vancouver.html)
Applicants for (Austrian) National Visa D have to book an appointment at VFS Global – Vancouver or Edmonton.
- For latest updates
 CLICK HERE (https://www.bmeia.gv.at/en/austrian-embassy-ottawa/travel-information-on-austria)

## Photo Specification
Please provide one recent (not older than 6 months) colour photograph of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
																					
- Taken against a plain light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Photograph attached (glued) to the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements (see examples here), then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application centre

## Processing Time
- Processing time for visa applications is minimum 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted.
- This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate.
- During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least three weeks prior to the date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.
- Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
